subject: Accounting
title: Earnings Per Share
description: A micro-lesson that makes Earnings Per Share usable.

hook.text: A company earns $100 million. Great—unless there are a billion shares. Then it's just $0.10 each.

buildup.text: Earnings per share divides net income by the number of outstanding shares.

discovery.text: EPS lets investors compare profitability across companies of vastly different sizes.

twist.text: Companies can boost EPS by buying back shares—fewer shares means higher per-share earnings.

climax.text: Share buybacks don't create real profit—they just redistribute it among fewer owners.

punchline.text: EPS tells you your slice. Not the size of the pie.

quiz.question: How is earnings per share calculated?
options: Net income divided by shares outstanding
